question: Which analytical technique is most commonly used for high-resolution separation and quantification of amino acid enantiomers in biological samples?
options: Standard reverse-phase HPLC with UV detection
Chiral HPLC or capillary electrophoresis with chiral selectors
explanation: Chiral HPLC and capillary electrophoresis with chiral selectors (such as cyclodextrin-based buffers) can resolve D- and L-amino acid enantiomers, which is critical for analyzing non-proteinogenic amino acids in biological samples.
